meta.description: System design interview questions commonly associated with Google SWE interviews

questions.title: Design Google Search
questions.description: Design a web search engine that crawls the internet, indexes billions of web pages, and serves relevant results with sub-second latency.
key_discussion_points: Web crawler architecture: politeness, URL frontier, deduplication
Inverted index construction and storage
Ranking: PageRank, relevance scoring, learning-to-rank
Query parsing and intent understanding
Serving architecture: sharding the index across thousands of machines
Caching strategy for popular queries
Snippet generation and result rendering
Freshness: incremental crawling and real-time indexing

expected_depth_by_level.L4: Core crawler-indexer-ranker pipeline, inverted index basics, simple ranking signals, query serving path
expected_depth_by_level.L5: Distributed index sharding, PageRank computation at scale, caching layers, freshness vs completeness trade-offs, query understanding pipeline
expected_depth_by_level.L6: Global multi-datacenter architecture, real-time indexing pipeline, personalization and localization, search quality feedback loops, capacity planning at planetary scale

questions.title: Design YouTube
questions.description: Design a video-sharing platform that supports uploading, processing, storing, and streaming billions of videos to users worldwide.
key_discussion_points: Video upload and transcoding pipeline (multiple resolutions, formats)
Video storage: blob storage for raw and processed video segments
Adaptive bitrate streaming (DASH/HLS)
CDN architecture for global delivery
Recommendation and home feed generation
Search and discovery features
Live streaming support
Monetization: ad insertion and creator analytics

expected_depth_by_level.L4: Upload pipeline, transcoding basics, blob storage, CDN-based delivery, basic recommendation
expected_depth_by_level.L5: Adaptive bitrate streaming, global CDN optimization, async transcoding pipeline with retries, recommendation system integration, live streaming architecture
expected_depth_by_level.L6: Multi-region content placement optimization, cost-efficient storage tiering, end-to-end latency optimization, platform-level decisions around live vs VOD infrastructure

questions.title: Design Google Maps
questions.description: Design a mapping and navigation system that provides directions, real-time traffic updates, and location search for users globally.
key_discussion_points: Map tile rendering and serving (vector vs raster tiles)
Routing engine: graph-based shortest path (Dijkstra, A*, contraction hierarchies)
Real-time traffic integration and ETA computation
Geocoding and reverse geocoding services
Points of interest (POI) search and indexing
Location data ingestion from multiple sources (GPS probes, street view, satellite)
Offline maps and data synchronization
Multi-modal routing (driving, walking, transit, cycling)

expected_depth_by_level.L4: Tile serving basics, graph-based routing with Dijkstra/A*, geocoding API, basic traffic overlay
expected_depth_by_level.L5: Contraction hierarchies for fast routing, real-time traffic aggregation pipeline, tile pre-rendering and caching, multi-modal route planning
expected_depth_by_level.L6: Global road graph partitioning, real-time rerouting at scale, predictive traffic modeling, map data pipeline from raw imagery to served tiles, offline-online consistency

questions.title: Design Gmail
questions.description: Design an email service that handles billions of emails per day with search, spam filtering, labels, and near-instant delivery.
key_discussion_points: Email receiving pipeline (SMTP ingestion, MX records)
Email storage: per-user mailbox storage and indexing
Full-text search over email content and attachments
Spam and phishing detection pipeline
Push notifications and real-time sync across devices
Attachment handling and virus scanning
Labels, filters, and smart categorization (Primary, Social, Promotions)
Data retention, archival, and compliance

expected_depth_by_level.L4: SMTP pipeline, per-user storage design, basic full-text search, spam filtering with ML, label system
expected_depth_by_level.L5: Distributed email storage with replication, search index sharding per user, real-time sync protocol, smart categorization ML pipeline, privacy and compliance considerations

questions.title: Design Google Drive
questions.description: Design a cloud file storage and synchronization service that allows users to store, share, and collaborate on files across devices.
key_discussion_points: File upload and chunked transfer for large files
Storage backend: metadata store vs blob store separation
File versioning and revision history
Sync protocol: detecting and resolving conflicts across devices
Sharing and permissions model (ACLs, link sharing)
Deduplication at the block level
Quota management and storage tiering
Notifications for shared file changes

expected_depth_by_level.L4: Chunked upload, metadata vs blob storage, basic sync protocol, file versioning
expected_depth_by_level.L5: Conflict resolution strategies, block-level deduplication, efficient delta sync, permission model with cascading ACLs, notification pipeline
expected_depth_by_level.L6: Global consistency for shared files, cost-optimized storage tiering (hot/warm/cold), cross-region replication strategy, enterprise compliance and DLP integration

questions.title: Design Google Docs (Real-Time Collaboration)
questions.description: Design a real-time collaborative document editing system where multiple users can simultaneously edit the same document.
key_discussion_points: Real-time collaboration: Operational Transformation (OT) vs CRDTs
Cursor and selection presence for multiple editors
Document storage model (structured content vs plain text)
WebSocket-based real-time communication layer
Conflict resolution and consistency guarantees
Revision history and undo/redo across collaborators
Offline editing and reconciliation on reconnect
Access control and real-time permission enforcement

expected_depth_by_level.L5: OT or CRDT fundamentals, WebSocket-based sync architecture, document model design, cursor presence, revision history
expected_depth_by_level.L6: Deep OT vs CRDT trade-offs, consistency guarantees under network partitions, offline editing reconciliation, cross-document linking, platform-level infrastructure for multiple doc types

questions.title: Design Google Photos
questions.description: Design a photo and video storage service with automatic organization, search by content, and intelligent features like face grouping and memory creation.
key_discussion_points: Photo/video upload, processing, and thumbnail generation pipeline
Storage optimization: compression, deduplication, tiered storage
ML-powered features: face detection/clustering, object recognition, scene classification
Search by content (people, places, objects, text in photos)
Auto-created albums, collages, and memories
Sharing and collaborative albums
Sync across devices with bandwidth optimization
Storage quota management and quality tiers (original vs compressed)

expected_depth_by_level.L4: Upload pipeline, thumbnail generation, basic storage design, face detection integration, content-based search
expected_depth_by_level.L5: ML pipeline for feature extraction at scale, embedding-based search, face clustering across user library, deduplication, bandwidth-aware sync
expected_depth_by_level.L6: Cost-optimized storage at exabyte scale, ML model serving infrastructure, privacy-preserving on-device processing, cross-product photo platform

questions.title: Design Google Translate
questions.description: Design a machine translation service that translates text, documents, web pages, and real-time speech across 100+ languages.
key_discussion_points: Translation API design: text, document, web page, and speech input modes
ML model serving: transformer-based NMT models for 100+ language pairs
Language detection as a preprocessing step
Pivot language strategy vs direct translation for low-resource pairs
Real-time speech translation pipeline (ASR + MT + TTS)
Caching frequently translated phrases and segments
Model management: per-language-pair models vs multilingual models
Quality estimation and user feedback collection

expected_depth_by_level.L4: Core API design, model serving architecture, language detection, caching strategy for common translations
expected_depth_by_level.L5: Multilingual vs per-pair model trade-offs, low-resource language strategies, real-time speech pipeline, model versioning and rollout
expected_depth_by_level.L6: Platform-level ML infrastructure for 100+ languages, quality-cost optimization, on-device translation for offline use, speech-to-speech latency optimization, community-driven translation improvement loops
